title: Protasparagus retrofractus
taxonomy: Protasparagus retrofractus is classified as a species of asparagus, specifically within the Asparagaceae family. It is native to Africa and is a climbing perennial plant. The exact classification of Protasparagus retrofractus may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a distinct species within the genus Protasparagus. Protasparagus retrofractus is further classified as a member of the Asparagales order and the monocotyledonous angiosperm family Asparagaceae. The classification of Protasparagus retrofractus is still a subject of ongoing research and refinement in the field of botany.

intro: Protasparagus retrofractus, also known as Asian asparagus, is a perennial plant species in the Asparagus genus, native to East Asia. The health benefits of Protasparagus retrofractus include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various conditions such as hypertension and arthritis. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
